basics.name: Michal Skupny
basics.label: Automation Engineer & PLC Programmer | Assistant at Poznan University of Technology

work.position: Assistant — Institute of Robotics and Machine Intelligence
work.summary: Teaching laboratory courses in industrial automation at the Department of Control and Industrial Electronics. Courses delivered in Polish and English for full-time and part-time students of Automatic Control and Robotics.
highlights: Programmable Logic Controllers — S7-1200, TIA Portal, LAD/SCL, motion control, analog I/O, PID controllers
Selected Applications of Programmable Controllers — HMI design, Ethernet communication, alarm handling, safety functions
Laboratory of Automation Elements and Devices — relay logic, pneumatic systems, sensor-based sorting lines
Development of original laboratory materials and exercise scenarios

work.summary: Engineering and programming of industrial automation systems for production lines and individual machines across textile, automotive, logistics and municipal sectors.
highlights: PLC and safety controller programming (Siemens S7-1200, S7-1500, S7-400 with Safety CPUs)
HMI visualization design and SCADA system development
Machine and production line safety concept development and auditing
Drive commissioning and parameterization (Sinamics over PROFINET)
Multi-protocol integration: Modbus TCP, Modbus RTU, analog I/O, PROFINET
IT/OT integration: Python-based PLC data acquisition (snap7), SQL database migration scripts
As-built documentation preparation: operator manuals, safety instructions, post-implementation reports
On-site commissioning, subcontractor coordination, permit management

work.summary: Maintenance of production machinery on a vehicle assembly line. Scheduled inspections, fault diagnosis and repair to ensure production continuity.
highlights: Preventive and corrective maintenance of PLC-controlled production equipment
PLC program modifications and SCADA/HMI alarm management
Cross-functional collaboration with production and engineering teams

education.thesis: Sensorless control of PMSM fed through a sinusoidal filter
